title: cyber_attack
description: A coordinated phishing and ransomware attack targets staff systems.

timeline.title: Suspicious Activity Detected
timeline.description: Multiple users report a fake payroll email with embedded links. Do not click any links.

timeline.title: Credential Compromise
timeline.description: Security detects logins from unusual locations. Reset passwords for affected accounts immediately.

timeline.title: System Isolation Required
timeline.description: Ransomware detected on workstation. Isolate affected systems from network to prevent spread.

timeline.title: Backup Verification
timeline.description: Critical files encrypted in shared drives. Verify backup integrity and offline backup status.

timeline.title: Incident Response Team Activation
timeline.description: Activate cybersecurity incident response team. Document all affected systems and begin forensics.

timeline.title: Recovery Initiation
timeline.description: Containment successful. Begin restoration from verified backups. Monitor for additional threats.

timeline.title: Post-Incident Review
timeline.description: Systems restored. Conduct lessons learned session and update security protocols.

node-1.title: Suspicious Email Received
node-1.situation: Multiple staff members have received a convincing fake payroll email containing embedded links. Some employees are asking whether to click it.
node-1.question: What is your first action?

options.label: A
options.text: Alert all staff via a broadcast message — do not click any links and report the email to IT.

options.label: B
options.text: Click the link yourself to investigate whether it is malicious.

options.label: C
options.text: Delete the email and assume staff will handle it themselves.

node-2.title: Credentials Compromised
node-2.situation: IT Security detects logins from unusual geographic locations, indicating staff credentials have been stolen.
node-2.question: How do you respond?

options.label: A
options.text: Force password resets for all affected accounts and enable multi-factor authentication.

options.label: B
options.text: Monitor the unusual logins to gather more information before acting.

node-3.title: Ransomware Detected
node-3.situation: Ransomware has been detected on a workstation. Files are being encrypted and the infection could spread across the network.
node-3.question: What action do you take?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately disconnect the affected workstation from the network to contain the spread.

options.label: B
options.text: Pay the ransom demand to recover the files quickly.

node-4.title: Recovery Phase
node-4.situation: The ransomware has been contained. IT has verified that offline backups are intact and unaffected.
node-4.question: How do you proceed with recovery?

options.label: A
options.text: Restore systems from verified clean backups and conduct a full forensic review.

options.label: B
options.text: Bring systems back online immediately to minimize downtime without full verification.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: All systems have been restored from clean backups. A lessons-learned session has been scheduled and updated security protocols are in place.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
